remaining_tasks.name: Prove M(s)>0 for Re(s)>1 from Euler product (may be feasible for sigma>1)
Connect M>0 to Levinson's method — is pointwise M>0 provable for sigma>3/4?
Test M(s) at heights t > 10^5 using higher Odlyzko zeros

decisions.decision: RH equivalent to Stieltjes property of T(w)
decisions.rationale: T(w) = sum 1/(w-gamma_n^2) is Stieltjes iff all poles real positive iff gamma_n real iff RH. Non-circular: T computable from xi'/xi via mpmath.

decisions.decision: RH equivalent to M(s) > 0 for Re(s)>1/2, Im(s)>0
decisions.rationale: M = Im[(s-1/2)*conj(xi'/xi)] > 0. Decomposition: Gamma term (~t*log t) vs zeta term (~sqrt(t)*log t). Gamma wins by ~10% at sigma=0.6.

decisions.decision: Known bounds on zeta'/zeta are 20x too weak to prove M>0
decisions.rationale: Standard bound |zeta'/zeta| << log(t)/epsilon gives ratio epsilon/2 ~ 0.05, but actual ratio is ~1.1. The bound ignores angular cancellation in the specific linear combination.

decisions.decision: The gap is average vs pointwise argument control (Levinson generalization)
decisions.rationale: Levinson controls arg(xi) on average -> 1/3 of zeros on line. M>0 requires pointwise control -> all zeros on line. The bridge is the content of RH.

next_action: Two viable next steps: (1) Try to prove M(s)>0 for sigma>1 from the Euler product — this is feasible because zeta'/zeta is bounded there. Would establish M>0 in half the domain. (2) Connect M-function to Levinson's method and check whether partial results (sigma > 3/4) are provable with current technology. Either extends the verified region analytically.
context_notes: Session 16 was a marathon: 15+ commits across dBN (closed), Jacobi matrix (new direction), and M-function (cleanest formulation). The M-function M(s) = t*Re(xi'/xi) - (sigma-1/2)*Im(xi'/xi) > 0 is the single inequality equivalent to RH, with operator-theoretic origin (Stieltjes = self-adjoint). Verified at 4000+1800 points. Decomposition shows Gamma dominates zeta by ~10% at sigma=0.6 — tantalizingly close but existing bounds are 20x too weak. The path forward is angular control of zeta'/zeta (generalized Levinson). Environment: /c/Python313/python.exe.
